name: Sealed-Intent Multi-Agent Coordination
description: Sealed-bid commit-reveal coordination among AI agents: agents COMMIT sealed (hashed) bids for an award (a compute slot / task assignment) before a REVEAL phase, so no agent can peek at or front-run another's bid. The commit phase is ON-LEDGER — an auction is a factory-born sovereign cell whose installed CellProgram IS the auction policy: each commit slot is WriteOnce (overwriting a committed bid is an EXECUTOR REFUSAL, the anti-front-running tooth) and the PHASE is anti-rollback (Monotonic floor + StrictMonotonic advance on close_commit/resolve). The winning bid SETTLES atomically through the verified per-asset executor (dregg-intent::verified_settle, the Rust mirror of the Lean Ring.settleRing / SealedAuction.settle). Guarantees proved in metatheory/Dregg2/Intent/SealedAuction.lean: a commitment opens to exactly its bid (no late-switching, under collision-resistance), no reveal before the commit phase closes, a non-committed party cannot settle, and the award is atomic and value-neutral.
state_constraints: WriteOnce(COMMIT_BASE + i)  // the anti-front-running commit board — each committed sealed bid is frozen
Monotonic(PHASE_SLOT)  // the anti-rollback floor (the phase may never rewind), on every method
MethodIs(close_commit) => StrictMonotonic(PHASE_SLOT)  // COMMIT -> REVEAL, strictly advancing
MethodIs(resolve) => StrictMonotonic(PHASE_SLOT)  // REVEAL -> RESOLVED, strictly advancing

deos.mount: register_deos (src/lib.rs) — folds the composed DeosApp into the StarbridgeAppContext affordance registry and SEEDS the auction cell (installs the auction program, PHASE = COMMIT); DeosApp::mount yields the axum router
deos.publish: Signature (the auction cell is a dregg:// sturdyref a bidder/auditor on another federation reacquires)
deos.on_ledger_commit_board: THE PAYOFF — the commit phase is ON-LEDGER: a WriteOnce commit-board cell (slots COMMIT_BASE..STATE_SLOTS). A sealed bid is FROZEN the instant it is committed, so OVERWRITING a committed bid is an EXECUTOR REFUSAL (WriteOnce), not an in-process BTreeMap membership check. This is the anti-front-running guarantee, lifted from a Rust check into the verified executor.

affordances.view_auction: cap-only, Signature (an OBSERVER reads the board)
affordances.commit_bid: GATED (cap∧state), Either — COMMIT precondition (PHASE==COMMIT); the fire writes the next free WriteOnce commit slot (read from live state), the executor re-enforcing the WriteOnce commit board (overwriting a committed bid is REFUSED)
affordances.close_commit: GATED (cap∧state), None/root — COMMIT precondition; advances PHASE COMMIT -> REVEAL, the executor re-enforcing StrictMonotonic(PHASE)
affordances.reveal_bid: GATED (cap∧state), Either — REVEAL precondition (PHASE==REVEAL); records the revealed value
affordances.resolve: GATED (cap∧state), None/root — REVEAL precondition; advances PHASE REVEAL -> RESOLVED and writes WINNER / HIGH_BID (frozen by WriteOnce), the executor re-enforcing StrictMonotonic(PHASE)

deos.seam: CLOSED — the fire runs against the seeded auction cell carrying the full auction program (the WriteOnce commit board + result registers + the Monotonic/StrictMonotonic PHASE lifecycle), so OVERWRITING a committed sealed bid (WriteOnce) and a PHASE that rewinds / does-not-advance (StrictMonotonic) BITE in the deos fire path itself (tests/deos_seam.rs). The factory-born cell carries the flat state_constraints predicate, so the anti-front-running and anti-rollback teeth are executor refusals on the born cell too (tests/factory_birth.rs).
